content:
You are maintaining an AI agent's MEMORY.md file. This file is injected into every AI context window, so the PERSISTENT output must stay lean and fit under {{max_size}}.

## Goal

Produce a true partition of the current MEMORY.md:
- PERSISTENT becomes the new MEMORY.md. It contains compact durable facts needed in future sessions.
- ARCHIVED is appended to the daily file for {{date}}. It contains the original detail that was moved out of MEMORY.md.
- Each fact or detail from the source appears in exactly one output section. If a detail is archived, leave only a short durable summary or archive pointer in PERSISTENT.

## What Belongs In PERSISTENT

Keep concise facts that remain useful regardless of when the next session runs:
- Current architecture, APIs, command surfaces, paths, URLs, credentials locations without secrets, and ownership facts.
- Stable preferences, rules, naming conventions, and operational constraints.
- Current project state summarized as outcome and next action, not the investigation story.
- Cross-system relationships that prevent future rediscovery.

Use compact bullets. Merge overlapping entries. Replace long historical sections with short durable summaries plus archive pointers.

## What Belongs In ARCHIVED

Move details that future sessions can retrieve from daily memory instead of loading every time:
- Session play-by-play, troubleshooting traces, command transcripts, old release state, and temporary blockers.
- Background stories that led to a durable fact.
- Long lists where PERSISTENT only needs the current canonical pointer or summary.
- Repeated facts already captured by a shorter persistent entry.

## Acceptance Criteria

- PERSISTENT is under {{max_size}}.
- PERSISTENT and ARCHIVED are a partition: content moved to ARCHIVED is absent from PERSISTENT except for a concise summary or pointer.
- PERSISTENT remains a valid MEMORY.md document with useful headings, but headings may be merged, renamed, or removed to meet the size target.
- ARCHIVED preserves moved detail well enough that daily memory search/read can recover it later.

## Output Format

Respond in EXACTLY this format:

===PERSISTENT===
(new MEMORY.md content, compact and under {{max_size}})

===ARCHIVED===
(details moved out of MEMORY.md, organized by topic)

{{activity_section}}---

## Current MEMORY.md Content

{{memory_content}}

variables.activity_section: Activity context section (jobs and chat sessions from the day, if any)
variables.max_size: Recommended maximum file size (human-readable, e.g. "8 KB")
